title: Orthochilus parvilabris
intro: Orthochilus parvilabris is a species of orchid commonly known as the "small-labellum orchid." This plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, cough, and respiratory problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can provide relief from congestion and promote overall well-being. The bioactive constituents of Orthochilus parvilabris include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Orthochilus parvilabris is often used to make herbal teas, infusions, and extracts, which are used to treat respiratory issues, while its essential oil is used in aromatherapy to promote relaxation and reduce stress.
